uplink.description: TTN example: line 1 in=72/out=200, line 2 zero, periods zero -> action.motion.count 272
uplink._note: Wire format from upstream vs133.js: line1 total_in ch0x03 type0xD2 uint32=72, total_out ch0x04=200; line2 total_in ch0x06=0, total_out ch0x07=0; line1 period ch0x05 type0xCC in=0/out=0; line2 period ch0x08 in=0/out=0. count = sum of all total_in + total_out = 72+200+0+0 = 272; detected = count>0. Expected authored against reference/upstream-codec.js channel decode.

uplink.description: Single line 1 with period traffic in=5/out=3, total in=40/out=37 -> count 77
uplink._note: Synthetic. line1 total_in 0x03/0xD2 uint32 LE [40,0,0,0]=40; total_out 0x04/0xD2=37; period 0x05/0xCC in uint16 [5,0]=5, out [3,0]=3. count = 40+37 = 77 (period counts are NOT added to the running total, only totals are). detected = true.

uplink.description: Multi-byte uint32 totals across two lines -> large count
uplink._note: Synthetic uint32-LE exercise. line1 total_in [0,1,0,0]=256; total_out [0,0,1,0]=65536. line3 total_in ch0x09 [16,0,0,0]=16; total_out ch0x0A=0. count = 256+65536+16+0 = 65808; detected true.
Synthetic boundary case. All totals zero -> count 0 -> detected false. Confirms detected is strictly count>0.
Synthetic. Empty byte array is rejected before the channel walk.

uplink.description: Unrecognized leading channel -> no recognized channels error
uplink._note: Synthetic. Channel 0xFF is not a VS133 counting channel; walk breaks immediately, nothing recognized.
